name: Donald Lambert
abstract: Donald "The Lamb" Lambert (February 12, 1904 – May 8, 1962) was an American jazz stride pianist born in Princeton, New Jersey, United States, perhaps best known for playing in Harlem night clubs throughout the 1920s. Lambert was taught piano by his mother but never learned to read music.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I finally got a chance to meet Donald Lambert once. They did one of these jazz festivals or something, at WNEW. They used to have them yearly in New York, and Donald Lambert was there and I met him

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: Of all the piano players that I've played with, I don't really say that a guy can't play... Donald Lambert, you know, James P. Johnson.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] That was Fats, that was James P., it was Duke, it was Donald Lambert, Willie The Loin Smith...

about_quotes.speaker: Happy Caldwell
about_quotes.evidence: this guy from over New -- named Donald Lambert -- see, he was a most underrated piano player.
